title: Processing Human Colon Muscle Layers for Single Nuclei RNA-seq Using PHOX2B Antibody Immunoselection
author: Christina M Wright
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.w3afgie
materials: Homogenize the lysate by vortexing for 30s.
Discard the column and keep the flow-through.
Follow the instructions on the Qiagen RNeasy Micro Kit for this section
Pre-cool the Dounce homogenizer and pestles on ice.
Pre-cool large and small centrifuges to 4 C.
Leave on ice for 15 minutes to block.
Invert the tubes ~5 times to wash.

steps.title: Half hour before the tissue arrives
steps.instruction: Half hour before the tissue arrives: Spray off forceps and scissors with RNase Away, and thoroughly rinse/soak in water Fetch dry ice. In the fume hood, add methylbutane to a petri dish on the dry ice. Place a few pieces of dry ice into the methylbutane to chill it.

steps.title: Transfer tissue, on ice, from the...
steps.instruction: Transfer tissue, on ice, from the hospital’s Pathology unit to your laboratory in sterile ice cold 1x PBS in a 50 mL conical or similar container. (~30 minutes)

steps.title: Upon arrival to lab, don appropriate...
steps.instruction: Upon arrival to lab, don appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) = (gloves, lab coat, face mask, face shield) .

steps.title: Pour cold 1x Dulbeccos Phosphate-Buffered Saline...
steps.instruction: Pour cold 1x Dulbeccos Phosphate-Buffered Saline and the tissue into a new sterile dish.

steps.title: Quickly trim off surrounding fat using...
steps.instruction: Quickly trim off surrounding fat using Dumont #2 forceps and a fine scissors.

steps.title: Then, using the fine scissors and...
steps.instruction: Then, using the fine scissors and Dumont #2 forceps, carefully cut submucosa and epithelium off tissue, leaving only muscularis.

steps.title: Cut the muscularis into small pieces (~5 x 5 mm)
steps.instruction: Cut the muscularis into small pieces (~5 x 5 mm).

steps.title: Label the appropriate number of cryomolds...
steps.instruction: Label the appropriate number of cryomolds with sample number and “muscularis+plexus.”

steps.title: Transfer the smaller tissue muscularis tissue...
steps.instruction: Transfer the smaller tissue muscularis tissue pieces into Cryomold Biopsy specimen molds half-filled with RNase Free Tissue-Plus O.C.T. Compound. Add O.C.T. to cover the tissue.

steps.title: Place the cryomold containing the cold O
steps.instruction: Place the cryomold containing the cold O.C.T.+plexus in the 2-Methylbutane in the fume hood.

steps.title: Wait 5 minutes for it to freeze
steps.instruction: Wait 5 minutes for it to freeze. Then transfer to a -80° C freezer in appropriate containment (e.g. plastic bags in labeled boxes).

steps.title: Record human subject information linked to colon specimen
steps.instruction: Record human subject information linked to colon specimen.

steps.title: Fill an ice bucket with dry ice
steps.instruction: Fill an ice bucket with dry ice.

steps.title: RNaseZAP a Dumont #2 Forceps
steps.instruction: RNaseZAP a Dumont #2 Forceps. Rinse with deionized MilliQ water.

steps.title: Transfer tissue labeled “Plexus” in biopsy...
steps.instruction: Transfer tissue labeled “Plexus” in biopsy cryomold on dry ice to Cryostat set to -20 °C.

steps.title: Freeze the tissue onto a metal...
steps.instruction: Freeze the tissue onto a metal cryostat mounting device using a minimum of O.C.T. compound.

steps.title: While waiting for tissue to freeze,...
steps.instruction: While waiting for tissue to freeze, label 3 DNA LoBind Tubes: “ Muscularis+Plexus”, “ Muscularis+Plexus”, and “ Muscularis+Plexus RNA”. Place these on dry ice or in the 20 °C for at least 3 minutes before sectioning so they have time to cool.

steps.title: Place Dumont #2 Forceps in cryostat to chill
steps.instruction: Place Dumont #2 Forceps in cryostat to chill.

steps.title: Mount the tissue, insert the blade,...
steps.instruction: Mount the tissue, insert the blade, and begin making 100 micron sections.

steps.title: Using the Dumont #2 Forceps, collect...
steps.instruction: Using the Dumont #2 Forceps, collect all sections in “ Muscularis+Plexus” tubes EXCEPT 2-5 sections. Put 2-5 random sections in tube labeled “ Muscularis+Plexus RNA”.

steps.title: Return the tubes with tissue to...
steps.instruction: Return the tubes with tissue to the -80°C on dry ice.

steps.title: Remove tubes labeled “RNA” from the -80
steps.instruction: Remove tubes labeled “RNA” from the -80. There should be one each for plexus and for surrounding muscle.

steps.title: Add 350 uL of buffer RLT plus to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 350 uL of buffer RLT plus to each tube.

steps.title: Run through gDNA spin column
steps.instruction: Run through gDNA spin column. Centrifuge for 30s at >=8000xg – I did 10,000g this time. Last time 8,000.

steps.title: Add one volume (usually 350 uL...
steps.instruction: Add one volume (usually 350 uL of 70% ethanol to the flow-through and mix well by pipetting up and down 4 times. Do not centrifuge, and proceed immediately to next step.

steps.title: Transfer sample, including any precipitate that...
steps.instruction: Transfer sample, including any precipitate that may have formed, to an RNeasy MinElute spin column placed in a 2 mL collection tube (find RNeasy columns in 4 degrees C freezer B). Close lid and centrifuge for 15s at > 8,000 g. Discard the flow thru

steps.title: Add 700 uL of buffer RW1...
steps.instruction: Add 700 uL of buffer RW1 to the RNEasy MinElute spin column. Clsoe the lid and centrifuge for 15s at 8,000 g (10,000 RCF). Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 500 uL Buffer RPE to...
steps.instruction: Add 500 uL Buffer RPE to the RNeasy MinElute spin column. Close lid gently, and centrifuge for 15s at >8000 g to wash the spin column membrane.

steps.title: Add 500 uL of 80% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 500 uL of 80% ethanol to the RNEasy MinElute column. Close the lid and centrifuge for 2 minutes at 8,000 g (10,000 RCF) to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the collection tube with the flow-through.

steps.title: Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column...
steps.instruction: Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column in a new 2 mL collection tube (supplied). Open the lid of the spin column, and centrifuge at full speed for 5 minutes to dry the membrane. Discard the collection tube with the flow-through.

steps.title: Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column in a new 1
steps.instruction: Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column in a new 1.5 mL collection tube (supplied). Add 14 uL of RNase free water directly to the center of the spin column membrane. Close the lid gently and centrifuge for 1 minute at full speed to elute the RNA.

steps.title: Analyze RNA on the Agilent RNA...
steps.instruction: Analyze RNA on the Agilent RNA 2100 Bioanalyzer with the Agilent RNA 6000 Pico Kit, along with any additional samples.

steps.title: Prepare Hoechst, 2.5 mg/mL
steps.instruction: Prepare Hoechst, 2.5 mg/mL: -Dilute 10 mg/mL Hoechst 1:4 in nuclease-free water. -Using 3 mL syringe and 0.22 micron filter, filter the Hoechst. -Store at 4 degrees C.

steps.title: Prepare Normal Donkey Serum for blocking
steps.instruction: Prepare Normal Donkey Serum for blocking: - Normal Donkey Serum in 10 mL of sterile 1x PBS. -Filter the Normal Donkey Serum using a 3 mL syringe and a 0.22 micron filter.

steps.title: Prepare Diluted Nonidet P40 substitute
steps.instruction: Prepare Diluted Nonidet P40 substitute: -Add 1 mL of RNase free Nonidet P40 substitute to 9 mL of RNase free water. -Place on rotator for half an hour to dissolve Nonidet P40 substitute.

steps.title: Autoclaving
steps.instruction: Autoclaving: -Rinse large iridectomy scissors and Dumont #2 forceps with RNaseZAP followed by MilliQ water. -Place in small autoclave bag. -Autoclave before use

steps.title: Reconstitute PHOX2B antibody
steps.instruction: Reconstitute PHOX2B antibody: -Reconstitute antibody as per the manufacturer’s instructions. -500 µL of sterile, RNase free PBS was added to 100 µg of antibody, to make a final concentration of 0.2 mg/mL. -Aliquot the antibody into 50 µL aliquots and store at -20 °C until use.

steps.title: Prepare Lysis buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare Lysis buffer: %s %s

steps.title: Prepare Staining buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare Staining buffer: %s %s

steps.title: Schedule FACS sort
steps.instruction: Schedule FACS sort: Schedule FACS sort on MoFlo Astrios or an equivalent sorter with a 70 micron nozzle and the ability to detect DAPI/Hoescht.

steps.title: Schedule 10x Genomics Sequencing run
steps.instruction: Schedule 10x Genomics Sequencing run: Schedule 10x Genomics Sequencing run in advance since samples are processed the day that nuclei are isolated.

steps.title: Prepare the appropriate quantities of Lysis...
steps.instruction: Prepare the appropriate quantities of Lysis buffer and Staining buffer (Steps 42 and 43) . Place them on ice.

steps.title: Rinse large iridectomy scissors and Dumont...
steps.instruction: Rinse large iridectomy scissors and Dumont #2 forceps with RNase Zap. Rinse with MilliQ water. Pre-cool large iridectomy scissors and Dumont #2 forceps on ice in an open 50 mL conical.

steps.title: Place a 6-well plate on ice
steps.instruction: Place a 6-well plate on ice. Fill wells with 6 mL of Lysis buffer.

steps.title: Get dry ice
steps.instruction: Get dry ice. Transfer sectioned human tissue to the bench on dry ice.

steps.title: Approximately 1 hour before FACS sort,...
steps.instruction: Approximately 1 hour before FACS sort, put on appropriate PPE (gloves, lab coat, face mask, face shield).

steps.title: Use Dumont #2 forceps to transfer...
steps.instruction: Use Dumont #2 forceps to transfer one Eppendorf tube with sectioned human tissue into 6 mL of Lysis buffer on 6 well plate.

steps.title: Chop rapidly with large iridectomy scissors for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Chop rapidly with large iridectomy scissors for 1 minute.

steps.title: Using a 1000 uL pipet, transfer...
steps.instruction: Using a 1000 uL pipet, transfer 2 mL of Lysis buffer + tissue/O.C.T. into a pre-cooled Dounce homogenizer.

steps.title: Homogenize tissue with 20 strokes of...
steps.instruction: Homogenize tissue with 20 strokes of the loose pestle and 50 strokes of the tight pestle. Note: homogenization often induces suction and the formation of foam. Cover homogenizer with a paper towel and wear appropriate lab coat and PPE during this step to avoid getting splashed. Suction is particularly likely to occur for these tissue pieces because there are so many muscle fibers.

steps.title: Put the tissue through a MACS...
steps.instruction: Put the tissue through a MACS SmartStrainer (30 micron) resting atop a 50 mL conical.

steps.title: Chase with 2 mL of Lysis...
steps.instruction: Chase with 2 mL of Lysis buffer to rinse the strainer. Switch out strainers if they appear to be getting clogged, between tissue samples, or after 3 uses.

steps.title: Repeat steps 54-59 until all Eppendorf...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 54-59 until all Eppendorf tubes with tissue from the patient have been homogenized. Homogenized nuclei from the same patient may be combined into the same 50 mL conical.

steps.title: If nuclei from a second human...
steps.instruction: If nuclei from a second human subject are being run that day, repeat steps 51-60. Note: Since homogenization and FACS sorting takes a long time, we never ran nuclei from more than 2 human subjects in a single day to maximize RNA quality.

steps.title: Spin down Lysis buffer+nuclei+O
steps.instruction: Spin down Lysis buffer+nuclei+O.C.T. on large centrifuge at 590 RCG for 8 minutes at 4 degrees C. Be sure to appropriately balance samples.

steps.title: After the centrifugation is over, use...
steps.instruction: After the centrifugation is over, use a Pipet-Aid to remove liquid from each 50 mL conical. Get the last bit of liquid out using a P1000. Note: you probably will not see a pellet at this stage. Be very, very careful not to pipet out the nuclei at the bottom of the 50 mL conical. I typically tilt the 50 mL conical to the side and leave a small quantity of liquid at the bottom.

steps.title: Resuspend nuclei from each patient in...
steps.instruction: Resuspend nuclei from each patient in 990 mL staining buffer by pipetting 5-10 times with a P1000.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of Normal Donkey Serum for blocking
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of Normal Donkey Serum for blocking.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of 2
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of 2.5 mg/mL Hoechst.

steps.title: For each human tissue sample, label three Eppendorf tubes
steps.instruction: For each human tissue sample, label three Eppendorf tubes: a.Isotype control tube b.Phox2b tube #1 c.Phox2b tube #2

steps.title: Transfer 330 µL of nuclei in...
steps.instruction: Transfer 330 µL of nuclei in blocking solution into each tube.

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.3 µL (3.3 µg) of goat IgG to the isotype control tube.

steps.title: Add 16
steps.instruction: Add 16.5 µL of PHOX2B antibody (3.3 µg) to Phox2b tubes #1 and #2.

steps.title: Attach Eppendorf tubes to Glas-Col Rotator with rubber bands
steps.instruction: Attach Eppendorf tubes to Glas-Col Rotator with rubber bands. Rotate samples at speed 30 in the cold room on a rotator for 45 minutes.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of 1x PBS...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 1x PBS (rinse buffer) to each tube.

steps.title: Spin in an Eppendorf centrifuge for...
steps.instruction: Spin in an Eppendorf centrifuge for 5 minutes at 400 RCF at 4° C.

steps.title: While spinning, prepare 1,300 µL of...
steps.instruction: While spinning, prepare 1,300 µL of 1:400 Alexa Fluor 647 Donkey anti-goat secondary antibody in staining buffer.

steps.title: When spin is over, pipette out...
steps.instruction: When spin is over, pipette out supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 400 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 400 µL of secondary antibody in staining buffer. Note: pipette sample 5-10 times until any residual clumps are gone.

steps.title: Rotate samples at speed 30 in...
steps.instruction: Rotate samples at speed 30 in the cold room rotator for 30 minutes. a.While waiting, label 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes, 1 tube per patient. Add 5 uL staining buffer to the bottom of each tube. These are the collection tubes. b.Also label Falcon round-bottom tubes, one tube per patient. These tubes will hold the sample to be sorted. c.Place labeled 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes and Falcon round-bottom tubes on ice to prepare for transport to FACS sorter.

steps.title: When 30 minutes is up, add...
steps.instruction: When 30 minutes is up, add 1 mL of 1x PBS (rinse buffer) to each tube with nuclei+secondary.

steps.title: Spin in an Eppendorf centrifuge for...
steps.instruction: Spin in an Eppendorf centrifuge for 5 minutes at 400 RCF at 4° C.

steps.title: Resuspend samples in 1 mL of staining buffer. Note
steps.instruction: Resuspend samples in 1 mL of staining buffer. Note: pipette sample 5-10 times until any residual clumps are gone. This is especially important to prevent clogging of the FlowMi strainer.

steps.title: Pipette the liquid into a P1000...
steps.instruction: Pipette the liquid into a P1000 pipette tip and attach the FlowMi strainer to the base. Strain through the FlowMi strainer into a spare Eppendorf tube. Note: if the FlowMi strainer clogs and liquid is not moving, do not panic! Slowly raise the plunger on your P1000 until it is fully raised. While holding the P1000 pipette tip, carefully remove the pipette tip from the P1000 pipettor. Then try to remove the FlowMi strainer from the base of the pipette tip with the lid of your Eppendorf while still holding the pipette tip securely. If you manage to do this successfully, pipette the liquid in the P1000 5 more times to thoroughly disrupt clumps, and try again with a new FlowMi strainer.

steps.title: Pipette the liquid into a new P1000 pipette tip
steps.instruction: Pipette the liquid into a new P1000 pipette tip. Strain through the top of the Falcon round-bottom tube by placing pipette tip flush against the strainer lid.

steps.title: Bring the samples and collection tubes to FACS on ice
steps.instruction: Bring the samples and collection tubes to FACS on ice.

steps.title: Run isotype control sample first to determine appropriate gating
steps.instruction: Run isotype control sample first to determine appropriate gating.

steps.title: Sort Hoescht+, PHOX2B+ nuclei (with higher...
steps.instruction: Sort Hoescht+, PHOX2B+ nuclei (with higher signal than isotype control) into the collection Eppendorf tubes with 5 uL staining buffer using 70 micron nozzle (this can sort up to 1,000 nuclei per microliter). Below is a sample image from a FACS sort. R7 were the sorted nuclei. R9 were determined to be PHOX2B negative by the isotype control.

steps.title: Save the FACS program for documenting...
steps.instruction: Save the FACS program for documenting how nuclei were selected for analysis.

steps.title: (Optional)
steps.instruction: (Optional). After FACS, combine 0.5 uL of the sorted sample in 9.5 uL Staining Buffer. Place 10 uL on a hemocytometer and view the nuclei under an epifluorescent microscope. Nuclei should be Hoescht+ and intact without large quantities of debris. Note: we routinely avoided counting nuclei numbers using the hemocytometer since our sorted nuclei counts were so low (1000-10,000) that a hemocytometer did not give a good estimate of nuclei number. Instead, we typically provided the estimate from the FACS machine.

steps.title: Samples are ready for 10x GEM...
steps.instruction: Samples are ready for 10x GEM generation and library preparation using the 10X Genomics platform.
